title: T-027 — cross_site_summary materialized view + pg_cron hourly refresh (§9.10, MS-001/003)
prompt:
# T-027 — cross_site_summary MV + pg_cron refresh

PRD: docs/prd/14-MULTI-SITE-PRD.md §9.10 (MV def), §10.1 MS-001/003, §15.3 (refresh <10s for 10 sites)
Project root: /Users/mariuszkrawczyk/Projects/monopilot-kira

## Goal
Pre-aggregated MV powering MS-001 Site Overview + MS-003 Cross-site Factory Aggregate dashboards.

## Implementation contract
1. Migration `packages/db/migrations/0052_cross_site_summary_mv.sql` per §9.10 DDL:
   - CREATE MATERIALIZED VIEW cross_site_summary AS SELECT org_id, site_id, ... FROM sites s WHERE s.active.
   - CREATE UNIQUE INDEX idx_css_pk ON cross_site_summary(org_id, site_id) — required for REFRESH CONCURRENTLY.
   - RLS not directly on MVs — wrapping VIEW v_cross_site_summary with RLS-aware filter (org_id = app.current_org_id()) AND (app.current_site_id() IS NULL OR site_id = app.current_site_id()).
2. pg_cron schedule: `SELECT cron.schedule('cross_site_summary_refresh', '0 * * * *', $$REFRESH MATERIALIZED VIEW CONCURRENTLY cross_site_summary$$);` (hourly).
3. Tests: MV populates from sample sites/holds/WOs; refresh CONCURRENTLY succeeds; wrapping VIEW respects RLS isolation; performance benchmark < 10s for 10 sites with 1M WO rows (pgbench).

## Files
- packages/db/migrations/0052_cross_site_summary_mv.sql [create]
- packages/db/src/schema/cross-site-summary.ts [create]
- packages/db/__tests__/cross-site-summary.test.ts [create]

## Acceptance criteria
<!-- AC count consolidated from 5→4 by Fixer F14a 2026-05-14 (no coverage lost) -->
1. Given migration 0052 applied, when \d cross_site_summary inspected, then 6 cols (org_id, site_id, as_of_date, wo_active, holds_active, inventory_value, avg_availability_7d) + unique idx on (org_id,site_id).
2. Given pg_cron jobs are listed, when queried, then 'cross_site_summary_refresh' is scheduled hourly.
3. Given 2 sites X and Y in org_A each have 1 active WO and 1 hold, when v_cross_site_summary is queried under withOrgContext(A) + withSiteContext(X), then only X's row is returned (RLS via wrapping view).
4. Given REFRESH MATERIALIZED VIEW CONCURRENTLY runs, when settled, then no exclusive lock blocked concurrent SELECT (proven by parallel-query test).
5. Given 10 sites with 100,000 work_orders each (seeded), when REFRESH runs, then duration < 10s (recorded via test harness measurement; failure logs runtime for tuning per §15.3).

## Test strategy
- RED: integration test asserting MV existence + RLS via wrapping view + concurrent refresh + perf threshold before migration applied.
- Run 'pnpm --filter @monopilot/db test:integration -- cross-site-summary'.

## Risk red lines
- Do not omit unique index on (org_id, site_id) — REFRESH CONCURRENTLY requires it.
- Do not put RLS on the MV directly — Postgres does not support RLS on MVs; wrap with VIEW.
- Do not exceed §15.3 10s budget on cold cache — if perf fails, consider per-site refresh partitioning.
- Do not schedule refresh more frequently than hourly without explicit perf review — pg_cron contention risk.
- Do not let MV stale data exceed 1 hour for live dashboards — surface staleness via as_of_date column.

out_of_scope: Do not implement dashboard pages here (T-028, T-029).
Do not implement P2 cross-region replication of the MV.

cross_module_dependencies.reason: pg_cron is a foundation primitive used here for hourly MV refresh.

Cross-mod dep to 00-foundation T-125 declared (withOrgContext + app.current_org_id() per F11 rule). withSiteContext composes on top of T-125; cross_site_summary wrapping view uses app.current_site_id() for site-scoped RLS filtering.

acceptance_criteria: Given migration 0052 applied, when \\d cross_site_summary inspected, then 6 cols + unique idx on (org_id,site_id).
Given pg_cron jobs listed, when queried, then 'cross_site_summary_refresh' scheduled hourly.
Given 2 sites X and Y in org_A each have 1 active WO + 1 hold, when v_cross_site_summary queried under withOrgContext(A)+withSiteContext(X), then only X's row returned.

Given REFRESH MATERIALIZED VIEW CONCURRENTLY runs, when settled, then no exclusive lock blocked concurrent SELECT; AND given 10 sites with 100,000 work_orders each when REFRESH runs then duration < 10s (§15.3).
test_strategy: RED: integration test MV + RLS via wrapping view + concurrent refresh + perf threshold before migration applied.
risk_red_lines: Do not omit unique index on (org_id, site_id) — CONCURRENT refresh needs it.
Do not put RLS on MV directly — Postgres lacks support; wrap with VIEW.
Do not exceed §15.3 10s budget — consider per-site refresh partitioning if slow.
Do not schedule more than hourly without perf review.
Do not let staleness >1h be silent — surface via as_of_date column.
